title: Specify rule frontmatter
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter block at the top of a rule markdown file. The schema applies only to the machine-readable frontmatter between the opening and closing `---` delimiters; Markdown body validation, including the required `## Rule` heading and duplicate-id checks across the first-party rule set, is handled by `specify lint framework`.

id.description: Stable rule identifier. V1 reserves UNI, SRC, FRAME, RUST, IFACE, SEC, OMNIA, VECTIS, and ORG namespaces with three-digit numeric suffixes. SRC namespaces shared source-adapter overlays; FRAME is reserved for framework declarative checks and is not used in consumer adapter codex.

severity.description: Default review severity for findings associated with this rule.

trigger.description: One-sentence condition that tells a generator or reviewer when the rule matters.

applicability.description: Small V1 applicability filter. These fields narrow where a rule is likely relevant; they are not suppressions, exceptions, or policy overrides.

languages.description: Lowercase language or format tokens such as `rust`, `typescript`, or `json-schema`.

artifacts.description: Closed artifact category tokens. Consumer-side tokens (`code`, `tests`, `contracts`, `specs`, `design`, `tasks`) compose with framework-side tokens (`skill`, `adapter`, `brief`, `reference`, `codex`, `rfc`, `doc`).

paths.description: Relative path or glob patterns. Absolute paths, URI strings, and `..` segments are rejected.

lint_mode.description: How this rule is expected to be linted. The lint runner decides execution; this field only classifies the rule.

rule_hints.description: Optional hints for future deterministic checks. V1 validates only the hint shape, not regex syntax or tool availability.

kind.description: Kind of deterministic signal the hint describes. Every kind in the enum is executed by the `specify lint` hint interpreter, which carries one `eval/<kind>.rs` interpreter module per `const`; no kind is reserved. Adding a new kind requires landing its interpreter in the same change.

value.description: Hint payload, interpreted by a future validator or review tool.

description.description: Optional human explanation for the hint.

config.description: Per-kind configuration carrying the policy values the engine reads (caps, allow-lists, override maps, expected sets, canonical paths) or relays to a referenced `kind: tool` checker. `regex` -> regexHintConfig; `reference-resolves` -> referenceResolvesHintConfig; `cardinality` -> cardinalityHintConfig; `unique` -> uniqueHintConfig; `set-coverage` -> setCoverageHintConfig or expectedOperationsHintConfig; `set-eq` -> expectedOperationsHintConfig; `fenced-block` -> fencedBlockHintConfig or fencedBlockContainsHintConfig; `constant-eq` -> constantEqHintConfig or constantFieldEqHintConfig; `presence` (file / markdown-section / directory-index) -> presenceHintConfig (the frontmatter selector carries no config); `field-grammar` (field-tokens / field-first-word) -> fieldGrammarHintConfig; `cross-reference` -> crossReferenceHintConfig; `cli-contract` (invocations) -> cliContractInvocationsHintConfig; `cli-contract` (event-ids / error-codes) -> cliContractIdsHintConfig; `cli-contract` (test-citations) -> cliContractTestCitationsHintConfig; `tool` (skill-body) -> criticalPathHintConfig or variableCoverageHintConfig; `tool` (links-registry) -> schemaRegistryHintConfig; `tool` (prose) -> numericCapHintConfig; `tool` (rules) -> nsOwnerHintConfig; `tool` (scenarios) -> catalogRunsHintConfig.

references.description: External or repository-local reference material that helps apply the rule.

label.description: Short display label for the reference.

deprecated.description: When present, marks this rule as deprecated while preserving the stable id for historical citations.

reason.description: Human-readable reason the rule is deprecated.

replaced_by.description: Replacement rule id, when there is a direct successor.

negative-match.description: When true, emit one finding per line where the pattern does not match.

capture-group.description: 1-based capture group index to parse as an integer for threshold comparison.

capture-op.description: Comparison operator applied to the captured integer.

suffix-must-not-start-with.description: Per-match filter: skip matches whose remainder starts with this literal prefix.

slash-skill-positional.description: When true, apply CORE-023 slash-skill positional semantics instead of matching `value` as a regex.

join-backslash-continuations.description: When true with `slash-skill-positional`, join backslash- and indent-continued rows before scanning.

referenceResolvesHintConfig.description: Optional `kind: reference-resolves` hint configuration. All values are policy supplied by the rule file, not the engine.

image.description: `markdown-link` source: when true inspect image embeds (`![alt](src)`); when false inspect plain `[label](target)` links.

target-suffix.description: `markdown-link` source: only consider targets whose path part ends with this literal (e.g. `.svg`).

target-prefixes.description: `markdown-link` source: only consider targets whose path part starts with one of these literals (e.g. `references/`, `examples/`).

path-prefix.description: `symlink` source: only consider symlinks whose path starts with this literal (e.g. `plugins/`).

cardinalityHintConfig.description: Optional `kind: cardinality` hint configuration. `max` is the inclusive upper bound a counted metric may reach; it is policy supplied by the rule, not the engine.

max.description: Inclusive maximum for the selected metric; values strictly greater are flagged.

uniqueHintConfig.description: `kind: unique` hint configuration. The field selector is policy supplied by the rule, not the engine.

field.description: Fact field whose value must be unique across the candidate set (e.g. `skill-name`).

expectedOperationsHintConfig.description: `kind: set-coverage` / `kind: set-eq` hint configuration for the `adapter-briefs` source (CORE-004 / CORE-007). The per-axis expected operation sets are policy supplied by the rule, not the engine.

expected-operations.description: `<axis> -> [expected briefs operations]` map. The axis tokens are the closed adapter axes; the operation lists are the policy.

sources.description: Operations a source adapter's `briefs.keys()` must declare.

targets.description: Operations a target adapter's `briefs.keys()` must declare.

setCoverageHintConfig.description: Optional `kind: set-coverage` hint configuration for the `skill-allowed-tools` source. Both the recognised-value set and the prefix exemptions are policy supplied by the rule, not the engine.

allowed.description: The closed set of recognised values a declared set must be covered by.

allowed-prefixes.description: Prefixes that exempt a declared value from the allow-list (e.g. `mcp__`).

fencedBlockHintConfig.description: Optional `kind: fenced-block` hint configuration for the `inline-json-too-long` source. The language allow-list and line cap are policy supplied by the rule, not the engine.

langs.description: Fence info strings this rule scopes to (e.g. `json`, `jsonc`).

max-lines.description: Inclusive maximum fence-body line count; longer fences are flagged.

fencedBlockContainsHintConfig.description: Optional `kind: fenced-block` hint configuration for the `fenced-body-contains` source. Both the language allow-list and the banned-substring set are policy supplied by the rule, not the engine.

langs.description: Fence info strings this rule scopes to (e.g. `text`).

substrings.description: Substrings whose presence in a matching fence body is a violation (e.g. flow-diagram arrows `->`, `→`).

constantEqHintConfig.description: Optional `kind: constant-eq` hint configuration for the `skill-name-plugin-prefix` source. The override map is policy supplied by the rule, not the engine.

overrides.description: `<plugin-dir> -> <required-prefix-base>` redirects for plugins whose skill-name prefix differs from the directory slug.

constantFieldEqHintConfig.description: `kind: constant-eq` hint configuration for the `adapter-manifest-field` source (CORE-006). Both the field selector and the expected value are policy supplied by the rule, not the engine.

equals.description: Value the selected field must equal (e.g. `1`).

criticalPathHintConfig.description: `kind: tool` (`skill-body`) configuration for the `## Critical Path` checks (CORE-040 / CORE-041). The line threshold and item bounds are policy supplied by the rule, not the tool. The engine relays this block to the tool verbatim.

min-body-lines.description: Body-line threshold at or above which a skill must carry (and correctly shape) a `## Critical Path` section.

min-items.description: Inclusive lower bound on Critical Path entries (CORE-040 only).

max-items.description: Inclusive upper bound on Critical Path entries (CORE-040 only).

variableCoverageHintConfig.description: `kind: tool` (`skill-body`) configuration for the `$VAR` coverage check (CORE-048). The built-in variable allow-list is policy supplied by the rule, not the tool.

builtin-vars.description: Variable names exempt from definition / reference coverage (e.g. `ARGUMENTS`, `HOME`).

fieldGrammarHintConfig.description: `kind: field-grammar` hint configuration. The `field-tokens` mode carries `{ field, token-pattern }` (CORE-035); the `field-first-word` mode carries `{ field, allowed }` (CORE-036). Each mode has a unique required key (`token-pattern` vs `allowed`) so the oneOf disambiguates; both require `field`. All values are policy supplied by the rule, not the engine.

oneOf.description: `field-tokens` mode: every whitespace-separated token of the named frontmatter field must match the grammar regex.

field.description: Frontmatter field whose whitespace-separated tokens are checked (e.g. `argument-hint`).

token-pattern.description: Anchored regular expression every whitespace-separated token of the field must match.

oneOf.description: `field-first-word` mode: the first alphabetic word of the named frontmatter field must be in the allow-list.

field.description: Frontmatter field whose first alphabetic word is checked (e.g. `description`).

allowed.description: Lowercase words the field's first alphabetic word may equal (e.g. the imperative-verb allow-list).

crossReferenceHintConfig.description: `kind: cross-reference` hint configuration. `target` names the target fact family the source (the hint `value`) is joined against; the required `target` key disambiguates the oneOf. With a fact-family source (e.g. `value: adapter-dir`) the join is presence-only: source items with no corresponding target item are flagged. With the `value: expected-set` source the rule supplies the optional `entries` table (each `{ key, value }`) and the join is value-equality against the target on `key`. The source / target selectors and the join keys are mechanism; which family is which, and the expected entries / values, are policy supplied by the rule, not the engine.

target.description: Target fact-family selector the source is joined against (e.g. `adapter-manifest` for a presence join, `adapter-tool` for a value-equality join).

entries.description: Expected-set rows for the `value: expected-set` source. Each row's `key` is joined against the target family; the matched target value must equal `value`. Absent for fact-family sources.

key.description: Join key composed against the target family's documented key mechanism (e.g. `<adapter-dir>/<tool>` for `adapter-tool`).

value.description: Value the matched target entry must equal (e.g. the pinned tool version).

cliContractInvocationsHintConfig.description: `kind: cli-contract` configuration for the `invocations` selector. The fence language set and the offending-token exemptions are policy supplied by the rule, not the engine; the contract itself is injected by the running binary.

langs.description: Fence info strings whose bodies are scanned for `specify …` command lines (e.g. `bash`, `sh`, `console`).

ignore.description: Offending tokens (verbs or `--flags`) exempted from findings (e.g. verbs that are illustrative, not shipped).

cliContractIdsHintConfig.description: `kind: cli-contract` configuration for the `event-ids` / `error-codes` selectors. The probed JSON field names and every exemption are policy supplied by the rule, not the engine; the id lists come from the binary-injected contract.

json-fields.description: JSON field names whose string values are membership-checked in fenced bodies (e.g. `event` for journal lines, `code` for error envelopes).

ignore.description: Exact tokens exempted from findings (e.g. dotted YAML paths that are not event ids).

ignore-suffixes.description: Suffix exemptions for the `event-ids` inline-span scan (e.g. `.yaml`, `.md` — dotted tokens that are file names, not ids).

allow-prefixes.description: Prefix exemptions for dynamically composed id families.

cliContractTestCitationsHintConfig.description: `kind: cli-contract` configuration for the `test-citations` selector. The link-target prefixes and exemptions are policy supplied by the rule, not the engine; the tests/ inventory comes from the binary-injected contract.

link-prefixes.description: Link-target prefixes that root a citation of the CLI repo's tests/ tree (e.g. the blob/ and tree/ GitHub URL prefixes).

ignore.description: Exact cited paths exempted from findings (e.g. downstream generated-project test layouts that are not CLI tests).

schemaRegistryHintConfig.description: `kind: tool` (`links-registry`) configuration for the brief schema-URL check (CORE-018). The tool→schema registry is policy supplied by the rule, not the tool; the engine relays this block to the tool verbatim.

known-schemas.description: Per-tool schema-ownership rows. Each names a tool and the schema base names it owns; a `schemas.specify.dev/<tool>/<name>.schema.json` URL resolves only when `<name>` is owned by `<tool>`.

tool.description: Tool that owns the listed schemas (e.g. `vectis`).

schemas.description: Schema base names the tool owns (e.g. `tokens`, `assets`, `composition`).

numericCapHintConfig.description: `kind: tool` (`prose`) configuration for the documented numeric-cap drift check (CORE-024). Both cap values are policy supplied by the rule, not the tool; the engine relays this block to the tool verbatim.

description-cap.description: Skill description character cap that must appear in the embedded skill schema and the standards document.

body-cap.description: Skill body line cap that must appear in the standards document.

nsOwnerHintConfig.description: `kind: tool` (`rules`) configuration for the namespace-ownership check (CORE-009). The owner->allowed-prefix map, the source-axis prefixes, and the reserved-namespace owners are all policy supplied by the rule, not the tool; the engine relays this block to the tool verbatim.

owner-prefixes.description: `<rules-directory-owner> -> [allowed id-namespace prefixes]` map (e.g. `omnia -> [OMNIA, RUST, SEC]`). The directory->owner derivation is mechanism in the tool; this map is the policy it consults.

source-axis-prefixes.description: Id-namespace prefixes every source-adapter rules directory may use (e.g. `SRC`). Source owners are discovered dynamically from the tree at runtime.

reserved-namespaces.description: `<reserved namespace> -> <sole owner>` map (e.g. `FRAME -> universal`). A rule whose id carries a reserved namespace under any other owner is flagged.

catalogRunsHintConfig.description: `kind: tool` (`scenarios`) configuration for the catalog↔runs drift check (CORE-056). The catalog path, the scenario / run directories, the legal Status and Gate value sets, and the status↔result agreement map are policy supplied by the rule, not the tool; the engine relays this block to the tool verbatim.

catalog.description: Project-relative path of the catalog markdown whose group tables are parsed (id from each row's File link).

scenarios-dir.description: Project-relative directory holding one `<id>.md` per scenario (README.md excluded).

runs-dir.description: Project-relative directory holding committed `<id>.<result>.md` run records (README.md excluded).

gates.description: Legal Gate column values (e.g. `release-blocker`, `full`). An empty list skips gate validation.

status-result-map.description: `<status> -> <record result token>` map for statuses that require a committed record (e.g. `passed -> pass`). Statuses absent from the map (e.g. `pending`) must have no record at all.

presenceHintConfig.description: `kind: presence` hint configuration. The `file` selector carries `{ path }`; the `markdown-section` selector carries `{ title, level, when: { metric, min } }`; the `directory-index` selector carries `{ roots, index, min-files? }`. The `frontmatter` selector carries no config. Each variant has a unique required key (`path` vs `title` vs `roots`) so the oneOf disambiguates. All values are policy supplied by the rule, not the engine.

oneOf.description: `file` selector: the single project-relative path that must exist on the tree.

path.description: Project-relative path the rule requires to be present.

oneOf.description: `directory-index` selector: each directory matching a root glob and holding enough files beneath it must carry the named index file directly inside it.

roots.description: Directory glob patterns selecting the corpus directories that owe an index. `*` does not cross `/` for this selector, so a pattern names one directory depth.

index.description: Index filename required directly inside each matched directory (e.g. `README.md`).

min-files.description: Require the index only when at least this many files live beneath the directory (recursive count; default 1).

oneOf.description: `markdown-section` selector: the required heading and the metric threshold that gates the requirement.

title.description: Heading text the required markdown section must carry (e.g. `Critical Path`).

level.description: Heading level the required section must use (2 for an H2).

when.description: Threshold gate: the section is required only when the named fact metric reaches `min`.

metric.description: Fact metric the threshold gates on (e.g. `skill-body-line-count`).

min.description: Inclusive threshold: at or above this metric value the section is required.

relativePathPattern.description: Relative path or glob (`*` does not cross `/`, `**` does, `{a,b}` brace alternation unions patterns). A leading `!` marks an exclusion path-pattern hint.
